problem_statement.title: Report base error class
problem_statement.body:
In #2283 we started entracing snapshots so that we get useful backtraces if snapshot code unexpectedly errors. This had the unfortunate side-effect of changing the reported class for expected errors. We now show the original message if it was an entraced error.

Fixes #2286.
problem_statement.issue_title: Error condition provided by `expect_snapshot` seems buggy in 3.3.0
problem_statement.issue_body:
```r
testthat::expect_snapshot(stop("error"), error = TRUE, cnd = TRUE)
```

```
── Snapshot ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
ℹ Can't save or compare to reference when testing interactively.
Code
  stop("error")
Condition <rlang_error>
  Error:
  ! error
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
```

The condition seems changed to `rlang_error` in 3.3.0.

Found in pola-rs/r-polars#1642.

patch:
diff --git a/NEWS.md b/NEWS.md
index fce396ec8..2901b040a 100644
--- a/NEWS.md
+++ b/NEWS.md
@@ -1,5 +1,6 @@
 # testthat 3.3.1
 
+* `expect_snapshot()` now reports the original error class for base errors, rather than `rlang_error` (#2286).
 * `expect_success()` and `expect_failure()` are more clear about what the expectation actually did (#2297).
 * The hint to use `snapshot_download_gh()` is now only emitted when running in a job named "R-CMD-check" (#2300).
 * `expect_snapshot_file()` correctly reports file name if duplicated (@MichaelChirico, #2296).
diff --git a/R/snapshot.R b/R/snapshot.R
index d95f4f2e8..971f895cf 100644
--- a/R/snapshot.R
+++ b/R/snapshot.R
@@ -185,12 +185,22 @@ snapshot_replay.condition <- function(
   }
 
   if (cnd_class) {
-    type <- paste0(type, " <", class(x)[[1]], ">")
+    type <- paste0(type, " <", error_class(x), ">")
   }
 
   c(snap_header(state, type), snapshot_lines(msg, transform))
 }
 
+error_class <- function(x) {
+  # If error was entraced from base R error, use original error class
+  # This is a little fragile because entrace() does not document this behaviour
+  if (inherits(x, "rlang_error") && !is.null(x$error)) {
+    x <- x$error
+  }
+  class(x)[[1]]
+}
+
+
 snapshot_lines <- function(x, transform = NULL) {
   x <- split_lines(x)
   if (!is.null(transform)) {
test_patch:
diff --git a/tests/testthat/_snaps/snapshot.md b/tests/testthat/_snaps/snapshot.md
index 35379f3f5..65b77b73f 100644
--- a/tests/testthat/_snaps/snapshot.md
+++ b/tests/testthat/_snaps/snapshot.md
@@ -243,7 +243,7 @@
     Condition <simpleWarning>
       Warning in `f()`:
       bar
-    Condition <rlang_error>
+    Condition <simpleError>
       Error in `f()`:
       ! baz
 
diff --git a/tests/testthat/test-snapshot.R b/tests/testthat/test-snapshot.R
index 4655b61f6..d6beb1243 100644
--- a/tests/testthat/test-snapshot.R
+++ b/tests/testthat/test-snapshot.R
@@ -169,6 +169,24 @@ test_that("errors and warnings are folded", {
 #   expect_snapshot(1 + 1)
 # })
 
+test_that("extracts original error class", {
+  catch_entraced <- function(code) {
+    tryCatch(
+      withCallingHandlers(code, error = function(cnd) rlang::entrace(cnd)),
+      error = function(cnd) cnd
+    )
+  }
+
+  cnd <- catch_entraced(stop("!", call. = FALSE))
+  expect_equal(error_class(cnd), "simpleError")
+
+  cnd <- catch_entraced(stop(errorCondition("!", class = "badError")))
+  expect_equal(error_class(cnd), "badError")
+
+  cnd <- catch_entraced(abort("!"))
+  expect_equal(error_class(cnd), "rlang_error")
+})
+
 test_that("hint is informative", {
   local_mocked_bindings(in_check_reporter = function() FALSE)
   withr::local_envvar(GITHUB_ACTIONS = "false", TESTTHAT_WD = NA)

fail_to_pass: test_that('extracts original error class', { ... }) - verifies error_class() returns 'simpleError' for entraced base errors
test_that('extracts original error class', { ... }) - verifies error_class() returns custom class for entraced custom errors
test_that('extracts original error class', { ... }) - verifies error_class() returns 'rlang_error' for native rlang abort() errors
Snapshot test showing Condition <simpleError> instead of Condition <rlang_error>
pass_to_pass: Existing snapshot tests continue to work correctly
Warning conditions still display correctly with their original class

key_changes: Add new helper function `error_class(x)` that checks if error was entraced from base R
If error inherits from 'rlang_error' AND has `$error` field, return class of `x$error`
potential_pitfalls: The `$error` field behavior is not officially documented by rlang, making this somewhat fragile
Need to handle both base R errors (simpleError) and custom error classes correctly
Native rlang errors (from `abort()`) should still show as 'rlang_error'
Must not break existing snapshot behavior for warnings and other conditions

instruction: Fix the bug in `expect_snapshot()` where base R errors are incorrectly reported as `rlang_error` instead of their original class (e.g., `simpleError`). When testthat started entracing snapshots for better backtraces, it had the side effect of changing the reported error class. Implement an `error_class()` helper function that extracts the original error class from entraced errors, and use it in `snapshot_replay.condition()` when reporting condition classes.
